name: ResourceName completeness invariant
description: Every ResourceName MUST exist for every active language. InsertLocaleStringResource enforces this by auto-creating 'Missing Translation' rows for all other languages on insert.
enforcement: Application-level in LocalizationService.InsertLocaleStringResource(). NOT enforced by DB constraint.
risk: If rows are inserted directly via SQL or bulk import, this invariant can be violated.

name: Unique index: (LanguageId, ResourceName)
description: UIX_LocaleStringResources_LanguageId_ResourceName enforces that each (language, key) pair is unique at the database level.
implication: BulkMerge import uses this as the merge key — safe because the index guarantees no duplicates.

name: Fail-open resource lookup
description: GetLocalizedResource returns the resourceKey itself (not empty string, not null) when the key is not found. This is an intentional design choice — the UI displays the key as a visible indicator of a missing translation.
source: LocalizationService: 'return resourceKey; // return the resourceKey instead of empty string'
implication: Missing translations are discoverable by seeing resource key strings in the UI (e.g. 'shared.DateFormatShort'). Not a runtime error.

name: CountryId = LanguageId equivalence
description: In this system, CountryId and LanguageId hold the same numeric range (1–6) and the same semantic mapping: 1=DK, 2=SE, 3=EN, 4=FI, 5=NO, 6=DE. They are used interchangeably in code.
risk: Coupling. Any future divergence (e.g. a 7th country without a corresponding language) would require significant refactoring.

name: Language list is static in production
description: There is no API to add or remove languages. Languages are seeded at DB setup time. The 10-hour cache on GetAllLanguages assumes the list rarely or never changes.
evidence: LanguageService only exposes GetAllLanguages(). No Insert/Delete. No admin API endpoint found for language management.
implication: Adding a 7th language requires a DB migration + reseeding LocaleStringResources for all existing keys.

name: Collation: Danish_Norwegian_CI_AI
description: Languages table Language.Name and LanguageCulture columns use COLLATE Danish_Norwegian_CI_AI — case-insensitive, accent-insensitive. LocaleStringResources uses server default collation.
note: Collation mismatch between Languages and LocaleStringResources was a flagged concern in system_model.json ('Collation on LocaleStringResources.ResourceValue and ResourceName'). ResourceName lookups are done with exact case (ToUpperInvariant in cache key) but the DB column does not enforce case sensitivity.
